name: Wormy Baked Apples
recipeInstructions:
1. Core the apples from the blossom ends, leaving the stem ends intact.
2. Mix together the raisins and chopped walnuts.
3. Stuff each cored apple with the mixture of raisins and walnuts.
4. Place the stuffed apples, stems up, in a 7x12-inch pan.
5. In a saucepan, combine brown sugar, water, butter/margarine, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Stir over high heat until the mixture boils.
6. Pour the hot syrup around the apples.
7. Bake uncovered at 350°F (175°C) for 30 to 35 minutes, basting occasionally with the syrup, or until the apples are tender when pierced and the skin begins to crack.
8. Remove the apples from the oven and let them cool in the pan for at least 10 minutes, or until they reach room temperature.
9. Set each apple in a small bowl and spoon syrup around the fruit.
10. In the top of each apple, cut a hole large enough for one candy worm and tuck one end into each apple, leaving most of the worm dangling.
11. Serve with cream poured over the apples. Makes 6 servings.
